name: a mages and Warrior's Desire
description:
In this world, emotion is not weakness.

It is power itself.

Every human being is born with a predetermined form of magic — some inherit flames, others storms, shadows, glass, gravity, light, blood, ash, or countless other manifestations beyond understanding. No one chooses their magic. It is decided from birth, woven into the soul long before a child ever learns their own name.

But magic alone means nothing.

Emotion is what gives it life.

The stronger the emotion, the stronger the manifestation becomes.

Fear sharpens shadows.

Sorrow deepens the sea.

Joy brightens light until it blinds.

Rage turns flames violent enough to melt steel.

A calm mage may summon only small waves beneath ordinary circumstances, yet once driven into grief or fury, entire oceans may rise alongside them. Magic does not care for morality, talent, or status.

It only answers emotion.

That truth shaped the entire world.

Cities stand protected behind enormous barriers raised by generations of mages. Entire wars have begun because one ruler lost control of their emotions for a single moment. Monsters roam the lands beyond civilization — creatures powerful enough to wipe out armies, forcing humanity to constantly fight for survival.

Yet magic users are not the only ones feared.

There are warriors.

Unlike mages, warriors do not release emotion outward into elemental manifestations. Instead, they turn emotion inward, transforming it into Aura.

Aura strengthens the body itself.

Speed.

Strength.

Instinct.

Durability.

The more intense the emotion becomes, the stronger the warrior grows.

A warrior consumed by rage may radiate crimson Aura so overwhelming that everyone nearby begins feeling traces of that same anger crawling into their own hearts. Fear can freeze entire battlefields. Determination can force exhausted soldiers back onto their feet. Some warriors inspire courage simply by standing still.

Emotion spreads through Aura like pressure crushing the air itself.

And because of that, warriors and mages have remained equals for centuries.

A
creator: forest_bono20193
